shaded region under curve between x=0 and x=1/2

point M lies on curve and is global minimum for x>0

value.shaded region: area under curve from x=0 to x=1/2, above x-axis

(a) Find the exact coordinates of $M$. [4]
(b) Find the exact value of $\int_0^a x e^{-ax} \, dx$. [5]

value: (b) Find the exact value of $\int_0^a x e^{-ax} \, dx$. [5]

Below diagram: descriptive sentence about curve and point M
Two subquestions (a) and (b) with mark allocations in brackets
Each subquestion followed by dotted lines for answer space
Curve starts at origin O, rises to a single maximum at M, then decays asymptotically toward x-axis

value.O: origin at intersection of x- and y-axes
value.M: peak of the curve, located in first quadrant

value: Point M lies above x-axis and to the right of y-axis

[4]

value.line_y_5: horizontal line intersecting curve at x=±2, but only x≥0 shown

Shaded region lies in first quadrant, x ≥ 0, 1 ≤ y ≤ 5
